theory_q1: The energy intensity is reported as 106.06 GJ/Cr. Rs. in FY2025, down from 124.74 GJ/Cr. Rs. in FY2024, indicating improved energy efficiency relative to turnover. Energy intensity adjusted for PPP is 219.12 GJ/million USD in FY2025, up from 130.05 GJ/million USD in FY2024, which may reflect currency adjustments. In physical terms, energy intensity improved significantly from 5.69 GJ/MT in FY2024 to 0.84 GJ/MT in FY2025. The data is audited and verified by the British Standards Institution (BSI) as part of 'Reasonable Assurance' for BRSR.
theory_q5: The entity has not implemented a Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) mechanism. However, some manufacturing units treat their wastewater internally using sewage treatment plants (STPs). The treated water is recycled and used for landscaping and irrigation purposes.
theory_q7: GHG emission intensity (Scope 1 and Scope 2) is 7.73 metric tons of CO2 equivalent per crore rupees of turnover in FY2025, down from 9.54 in FY2024. When adjusted for PPP, it is 15.97 metric tons of CO2 equivalent per million USD in FY2025, compared to 9.95 in FY2024. In physical output terms, it decreased from 0.43 metric tons of CO2 equivalent per metric ton of production in FY2024 to 0.06 in FY2025. The data has been independently assured by BSI Group India Pvt. Ltd.
theory_q8: The entity has implemented projects to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, primarily through the use of alternate energy sources. In FY2024-25, 62% of direct energy needs were met using bio-based fuels such as firewood, biomass pellets, spent tea waste, briquettes, and husk. Additionally, the ICD Theni facility commissioned a 500 KWp solar power plant, increasing its total solar capacity to 1.5 MWp. The company met 24% of its total power demand through renewable sources (solar and wind), reducing reliance on grid electricity and lowering indirect emissions.
theory_q10: The company follows the 3Rs approach—reduce, reuse, and recycle—for waste management. Hazardous and toxic chemicals have been eliminated from products and processes. Waste is sorted into domestic, hazardous, and industrial categories and disposed of responsibly. All beverage production facilities in India maintain zero waste to landfill status. The company complies with India's Plastic Waste Management Rules and has committed to collecting and recycling 100% of its plastic packaging waste under its Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) plan. Authorized vendors handle recycling or repurposing of materials.
theory_q12: The grievance redressal mechanism varies by employee/worker category. Permanent employees and non-permanent employees can raise grievances directly with their line manager or through an HR SPOC. Permanent workers can raise concerns via Workmen Representatives/Unions, with joint meetings held to resolve issues. Non-permanent workers' grievances are raised by contractors, with joint meetings and mandatory updates on resolution status. An open-door policy is in place where unions are not operational, with designated contacts such as floor supervisors and welfare officers. The Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) under the POSH Act is functional at all units for addressing sexual harassment complaints.
p3_ca_hand_injury: To address the significant risk of hand injuries, particularly from packing machines at operational sites, the company has implemented several corrective actions. These include establishing machine guarding compliance assurance protocols during the design phase, conducting regular compliance inspections, integrating engineering control interlocks, conducting operator briefings, providing personnel training, and promoting awareness through safety initiatives, toolbox talks, and health and safety timeouts.
